task_completion.description: Verify the parent page 'Digital Humanities Research Guide' is created and published with the correct content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress page editor or 'All Pages' list showing the title 'Digital Humanities Research Guide', the content containing 'digital humanities resources available to researchers', and the status as Published.

task_completion.description: Verify the first child page 'Getting Started with Digital Humanities' is created, published, assigned to the parent, has order 1, and contains the correct content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress page editor showing the title 'Getting Started with Digital Humanities', the content containing 'introduction to the field of digital humanities', the 'Parent' attribute set to 'Digital Humanities Research Guide', the 'Order' set to 1, and the status as Published. Alternatively, look for it in the 'All Pages' list under the parent.

task_completion.description: Verify the second child page 'Primary Source Databases' is created, published, assigned to the parent, has order 2, and contains the correct content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress page editor showing the title 'Primary Source Databases', the content containing 'primary source collections and archival databases', the 'Parent' attribute set to 'Digital Humanities Research Guide', the 'Order' set to 2, and the status as Published. Alternatively, look for it in the 'All Pages' list under the parent.

task_completion.description: Verify the third child page 'Digital Tools and Software' is created, published, assigned to the parent, has order 3, and contains the correct content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress page editor showing the title 'Digital Tools and Software', the content containing 'computational tools and software platforms', the 'Parent' attribute set to 'Digital Humanities Research Guide', the 'Order' set to 3, and the status as Published. Alternatively, look for it in the 'All Pages' list under the parent.

task_completion.description: Verify the fourth child page 'Citation and Attribution Guide' is created, published, assigned to the parent, has order 4, and contains the correct content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress page editor showing the title 'Citation and Attribution Guide', the content containing 'proper citation practices and attribution standards', the 'Parent' attribute set to 'Digital Humanities Research Guide', the 'Order' set to 4, and the status as Published. Alternatively, look for it in the 'All Pages' list under the parent.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress GUI to create and publish the pages.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard and page editor being used, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual WordPress application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard WordPress interface elements like the admin sidebar, publish button, and page attributes panel.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the expected output into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The content must be entered into the WordPress page editor, not a generic text editor like nano or gedit.
